title: Lycocarpus fugax
taxonomy: Lycocarpus fugax is classified within the family Cephalotaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the finger lily or brain fern family. The family Cephalotaceae is part of the order Saxifragales, a group of flowering plants that also includes saxifrages and crassulas. Within the Cephalotaceae family, Lycocarpus fugax is a unique species known for its distinctive features and growth habits. The classification of Lycocarpus fugax is further supported by molecular phylogenetic studies that have confirmed its placement within the Cephalotaceae family. As a member of the Saxifragales order, Lycocarpus fugax shares characteristics with other species in this group.
